_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
- **ACTION REQUIRED — Register the workspace with the IATA SAF Registry as an intermediary/freight forwarder account.** The SAF Registry was upgraded in September 2025 specifically to cater to freight forwarders and intermediaries, and further refinements in early 2026 now support CORSIA compliance — including automatic generation of the CORSIA Eligible Fuel form. With CORSIA's mandatory phase beginning from 2027, the workspace must establish its registry presence and transaction capability before carrier counterparties begin embedding SAF-attribution obligations in airway-bill and contractual terms. Deadline: **30 June 2026** (ahead of CORSIA First Phase compliance deadline in late 2027). *Sources: IATA SAF Registry/CADO, April 2026, https://airlines.iata.org/2026/04/14/ensuring-transparency-saf-environmental-benefits*

- **WINDOW CLOSING — Review all air-freight carrier contracts on Americas/Europe/Asia lanes for SAF cost pass-through and CORSIA fuel-claim clauses before CORSIA First Phase closes.** CORSIA's First Phase (2024–2026) ends 31 December 2026; airlines are projected to purchase 146–236 million Eligible Emissions Units (EEUs) for cancellation by late 2027 at an estimated sector-wide cost of $4–5 billion. Carriers will seek to recover surcharges from shippers. The workspace must audit existing agreements now and negotiate transparent SAF/CORSIA cost itemisation on airfreight invoices. Deadline: **31 December 2026** (end of CORSIA First Phase). *Sources: IATA CORSIA Fact Sheet, December 2025, https://www.iata.org/en/iata-repository/pressroom/fact-sheets/fact-sheet-corsia/; GreenAir News, 2026, https://www.greenairnews.com/?p=8325*

- **COST ALERT — Quantify SAF premium exposure across live events, fine art, luxury, and film/TV airfreight volumes.** In 2024 SAF production doubled to approximately 1 million tonnes globally but still represented just 0.3% of jet fuel consumption; supply remains critically short. Price premiums for SAF over conventional jet fuel remain significant. For cargo verticals with inelastic delivery schedules (live events, humanitarian), unplanned SAF surcharges represent a margin and client-relationship risk. The workspace should model premium scenarios against its top 20 trade lanes. Deadline: **within 30 days**. *Sources: ICAO A42 Working Paper WP/163, ICAO, 2025, https://www.icao.int/sites/default/files/Meetings/a42/Documents/WP/wp_163_en.pdf*

- **MONITORING — Track EU ReFuelEU Aviation enforcement penalties commencing 2026.** EU Member States will enforce penalties for the 2025 biofuel SAF mandate target (2% blend) during 2026. Non-compliance penalties for fuel suppliers are set at a minimum of twice the price differential between biofuels and conventional jet fuel, multiplied by the shortfall volume — approximately €2,700/tonne for biofuels — with costs potentially cascading into freight rates on EU departure airports. Monitor fuel-supplier communications at European gateway airports used for the workspace's luxury/fine art/live events lanes. Deadline: **ongoing; first enforcement actions expected mid-2026**. *Sources: Climate Catalyst / ReFuelEU Aviation analysis, March 2026, https://climatecatalyst.org/learning-hub/sustainable-aviation-fuel-policy-in-the-eu/*

- **COMPETITIVE EDGE — Develop a SAF offtake/book-and-claim offering for high-value cargo clients.** Luxury, fine art, and film/TV clients increasingly require scope 3 emissions disclosure. The workspace can differentiate by sourcing CORSIA-certified SAF book-and-claim certificates on behalf of clients, attributing verified emissions reductions through the IATA SAF Registry. This positions the workspace ahead of carriers who are not yet systematically offering attributed SAF to cargo clients. *Sources: IATA SAF Registry/CADO, April 2026, https://airlines.iata.org/2026/04/14/ensuring-transparency-saf-environmental-benefits; ICAO SAF page, April 2026, https://www.icao.int/SAF*
